title: rootful podman 3.4.6 on OL7 container w/crun: cant start container (in container) due to: error loading cached network config: network \"podman\" not found in CNI cache
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm running a built-from-source version of podman 3.4.6 on Oracle Linux 7 with built-from-source crun v1.4.4.   I am running 'podman run' in a Kubernetes (OKE) container as part of a CICD job.

When I start a podman container (inside the Kubernetes/OKE container) as root (via sudo podman), it fails with the error: 
 "error loading cached network config: network \"podman\" not found in CNI cache" and then everything starts getting cleaned up.   Exit status code is 126.     This error message seems to occur when setting up/calling CNI.

This message occurs even though the 'podman' network exists (as shown by podman network ls just before trying to start the container)

Command:
```
export CPN=serverintegrationdockerimagecreatemasterpodmanol7root-30
sudo podman volume create ${CPN}_DBu01

sudo podman run -p 8080 --no-healthcheck --runtime=/usr/bin/crun --log-level=debug --rm -v serverintegrationdockerimagecreatemasterpodmanol7root-30_DBu01:/u01 -u root <someimagepath>/oracle-database-19c/19c-19.3.0-ee /bin/bash -c 'chown -v oracle:oinstall /u01 && chmod -v 777 /u01 && stat /u01 && ls -l /u01'
```

Debug output showing error:
```
...
+ sudo podman run -p 8080 --no-healthcheck --runtime=/usr/bin/crun --log-level=debug --rm -v serverintegrationdockerimagecreatemasterpodmanol7root-30_DBu01:/u01 -u root <DOCKERREGISTRY>/oracle-database-19c/19c-19.3.0-ee /bin/bash -c 'chown -v oracle:oinstall /u01 && chmod -v 777 /u01 && stat /u01 && ls -l /u01'
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On RHEL7 or derivative (Oracle Linux 7), build podman 3.4.6 and dependencies from source:
```
sudo yum -y install https://vault.centos.org/centos/8/AppStream/x86_64/os/Packages/yajl-2.1.0-10.el8.x86_64.rpm  https://vault.centos.org/centos/8/PowerTools/x86_64/os/Packages/yajl-devel-2.1.0-10.el8.x86_64.rpm
sudo yum -y install iptables
sudo yum -y install https://yum.oracle.com/repo/OracleLinux/OL7/developer/x86_64/getPackage/slirp4netns-0.4.3-4.el7_8.x86_64.rpm

sudo mount --make-rshared /
sudo setenforce 0 
sudo yum -y reinstall shadow-utils

sudo yum -y install https://yum.oracle.com/repo/OracleLinux/OL7/olcne11/x86_64/getPackage/conmon-2.0.10-5.el7.x86_64.rpm
sudo yum -y --enablerepo=\* install curl \
  gcc \
  make \
  device-mapper-devel \
  git \
  go \
  btrfs-progs-devel \
  containernetworking-plugins \
  containers-common \
  glib2-devel \
  glibc-devel \
  glibc-static \
  golang-github-cpuguy83-md2man \
  gpgme-devel \
  iptables \
  libassuan-devel \
  libgpg-error-devel \
  libseccomp-devel \
  libselinux-devel \
  pkgconfig \
  systemd-devel \
  autoconf \
  python3 \
  python3-devel \
  python3-pip \
  yajl-devel \
  libcap-devel

(
  set -x
  rm -rf ~/go || true
  #### install GO
  mkdir -p ~/go
  pushd ~/go
  wget https://storage.googleapis.com/golang/getgo/installer_linux
  chmod +x ./installer_linux
  ./installer_linux
)

#### install conmon
cd
git clone https://github.com/containers/conmon
cd conmon
export GOCACHE="$(mktemp -d)"
make
sudo make podman
sudo make install

### install runc
cd
git clone https://github.com/opencontainers/runc.git $GOPATH/src/github.com/opencontainers/runc
cd $GOPATH/src/github.com/opencontainers/runc
make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp"
sudo make install
sudo cp runc /usr/bin/runc

sudo mkdir -p /etc/containers
sudo curl -L -o /etc/containers/registries.conf https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/containers-common/raw/main/f/registries.conf
sudo curl -L -o /etc/containers/policy.json https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/containers-common/raw/main/f/default-policy.json

#### build podman v3.4.6
cd
TAG=v3.4.6
rm -rf podman*
wget https://github.com/containers/podman/archive/refs/tags/${TAG}.tar.gz
tar xf ${TAG}.tar.gz
cd podman*/
make BUILDTAGS="selinux seccomp systemd exclude_graphdriver_btrfs exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper libdm_no_deferred_remove containers_image_openpgp"
sudo make install PREFIX=/usr
sudo podman version


### custom crun v1.4.4 for RH7 build here, following steps from https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/359 without systemd.

sudo tee /etc/containers/containers.conf > /dev/null <<'EOF'
[engine]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
runtime = "crun"
EOF

```
2. Create Volume, start container:
```
export CPN=serverintegrationdockerimagecreatemasterpodmanol7root-30
sudo podman volume create ${CPN}_DBu01

sudo podman run -p 8080 --no-healthcheck --runtime=/usr/bin/crun --log-level=debug --rm -v serverintegrationdockerimagecreatemasterpodmanol7root-30_DBu01:/u01 -u root <someimagepath>/oracle-database-19c/19c-19.3.0-ee /bin/bash -c 'chown -v oracle:oinstall /u01 && chmod -v 777 /u01 && stat /u01 && ls -l /u01'
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(podman was built from source, using tag v3.4.6)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

I did check the troubleshooting guide.   I did not test with podman 4.0 because I could not find a 4.0 release in RPM form.   This works on OL8 at podman v3.4.2

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
This is running in a CRIO-based Kubernetes/OKE container.

comments.body:
I've attached the output when running with strace:

[podman.strace.txt.gz](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8493215/podman.strace.txt.gz)

the container in which I am running podman does not have systemd.

/etc/containers/containers.conf has contents:
```
[engine]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
runtime = "crun"
```

crun at tip was custom-built using steps on https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/359 (with --disable-systemd).  I needed to use crun to get past a cgroup issue with runc.

```
# crun --version
crun version 1.4.4.0.0.0.19-3668
commit: 366877655b3cf96f1890b76d7f59afcb8be6390a
spec: 1.0.0
+SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +YAJL
```

> iptables v1.4.21: can't initialize iptables table `nat': Table does not exist (do you need to insmod?)

You have to load the iptables kernel module. Likely a problem with your old kernel or iptables version.

This is not a podman issue, if you need to get this running on such an old operating system you are on your own. It is best to just stick with the packages in the default repos since they should work.

thank-you.   This confirms why it works properly on OL8, which has a newer version of iptables (1.8.4).  OL7 has iptables installed (1.4).  The Kubernetes Node (HV) has iptables 1.8.4 as well.   I'm guessing rootful podman works properly on OL8 because the version of iptables matches between the Kubernetes Node and the container environment running podman.

Unfortunately, I dont see a way to run rootful podman v3.4 containers (in OL7) on kubernetes due to the requirement that rootful containers use iptables, along with this mismatch between iptables versions in the podman container (1.4) and the kubernetes node. (1.8.4).   (Building nft iptables 1.8.4 for OL7 is a non-trivial effort.)

Rootless containers with slirp4netns, using podman 3.4 are another option for OL7 -- no need for iptables.

Looking forward to trying out podman 4.0. :-)
